sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager or his designee to execute an
amendment to Contract No. 32300185 with JSI Research & Training Institute, Inc
increasing the contract by $25,000 to serve as a Community Health Assessment,
Innovation, and Improvement Plan consultant for the City of Berkeley’s Health, Housing,
and Community Services Department (HHCS) in a total amount not to exceed $274,413
with a contract end date of November 1, 2025.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The total not-to-exceed amount of this contract will be $274,413. Resources for the
amended contract will be provided by the Future of Public Health grant (Fund 302)
received by the City of Berkeley’s HHCS department from the California Department of
Public Health.
sections.current_situation:
The City currently has a contract with JSI Research & Training Institute, Inc. to serve as
a consultant on the Community Health Assessment, Innovation, and Improvement Plan
for the City of Berkeley’s HHCS Department. These consultant services include
conducting a landscape scan for partnership opportunities to reduce chronic racial
disparities and improve Berkeley's public health strategy, conduct a Community Health
Assessment (CHA), and develop a Community Health Improvement Plan (CHIP).
The current contract with JSI Research & Training Institute, Inc. expires on May 1, 2025
and this item requests that it be extended to November 1, 2025 to allow for completion
of contract deliverables. The contract was approved by Council on April 11, 2023 with
Resolution No. 70,754-N.S.
The HHCS Department’s health equity work, which includes completion of the
Community Health Assessment and Improvement Plan, is a priority project under the

Department’s Strategic Plan for Health Equity, advancing our goal to champion and
demonstrate social and racial equity and achieving health equity.
sections.background:
The State of California encourages all public health jurisdictions to complete a CHA and
a CHIP on a regular basis. These are prerequisites for public health department
accreditation, which has been discussed as a state priority.
JSI Research & Training Institute, Inc. entered into the current contract with the City on
May 1, 2023 to complete the City’s CHA and develop the City’s CHIP. They have
recently completed the CHA phase of the project, which involved the creation of a
community steering committee, development and implementation of a community
survey, and community focus groups. The CHA has been completed and JSI has
initiated the CHIP phase.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no identifiable environmental effects or opportunities associated with the
action requested in this report.
sections.rationale:
The process of completing the CHA took more time in identifying and working with the
Community Steering Committee and completing the report. It is anticipated that more
hours will be needed to complete the Community Health Improvement Plan phase.
In addition, HHCS staff has asked JSI Research & Training Institute, Inc. to provide
support to the department conducting outreach with HHCS leadership and other city
leaders to present and discuss the CHIP plans. They have also been asked to assist the
department in engaging the Alameda County’s Managed Care Plans (MCP) to identify
ideas for using the resources that the MCPs have dedicated to this process.
sections.alternatives:
If the Department is unable to amend and extend the contract with JSI Research &
Training Institute, Inc. completion of the CHIP is unlikely, or will be delayed.
CONTACT PERSON
Kellie Knox, Future of Public Health Coordinator, HHCS (510) 981-5301
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 2
Page 3 of 3
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT NO. 32300185 AMENDMENT – JSI RESEARCH & TRAINING
INSTITUTE, INC. FOR COMMUNITY HEALTH ASSESSMENT, INNOVATION, AND
IMPROVEMENT PLAN CONSULTANT
WHEREAS, on May 1, 2023, the Health, Housing and Community Services Department
entered into a contract with JSI Research & Training Institute, Inc. (Contract No.
323000185) serve as a consultant on the Community Health Assessment, Innovation,
and Improvement Plan for the City of Berkeley’s Health, Housing, and Community
Services (HHCS); and
WHEREAS, Contract No. 323000185 with JSI Research & Training Institute, Inc. was
approved by Council on April 11, 2023 with Resolution No. 70,754-N.S; and
WHEREAS, JSI Research & Training Institute, Inc. has partially completed this contract
and is requesting more staff hours and more time to complete it; and
WHEREAS, JSI Research & Training Institute, Inc. has provided necessary services
through the completion of the Community Health Assessment and continues their work
with the Community Health Implementation Plan.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED that the Council of the City of Berkeley
approves a resolution authorizing the City Manager or designee to amend Contract No.
32300185 with JSI Research & Training Institute, Inc. for consultation to add $25,000 in
Future of Public Health grant funding (Fund 302), for a total not to exceed amount of
$274,413, and extend the contract to November 1, 2025.
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the City Manager or his designee, is hereby
authorized to execute all original or amended documents or agreements to effectuate
this action; a signed copy of said documents, agreements and any amendments will be
kept on file in the Office of City Clerk.
